data.title: 'Defensible Moats for AI Security'? CrowdStrike Stock (CRWD) Plunges on Target Cuts
data.description:
Cybersecurity platform CrowdStrike's (CRWD) shares fell about 5% early Tuesday after two Wall Street analysts cut their price targets on the stock. The moves came as they reassessed the cybersecurity and enterprise software group following a nearly $1 trillion wipeout in global software names on fears of AI-driven disruption.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

CrowdStrike and Zscaler Have 'Defensible Moats,' Says Truist

CrowdStrike's shares plunged despite Truist analyst Junaid Siddiqui noting that the Texas-based company, alongside peer Zscaler (ZS), offers "defensible moats for AI security." This implies that both firms are well-positioned with their AI-driven security offerings and should benefit rather than be displaced by artificial intelligence innovation in the industry.

Siddiqui reaffirmed his Buy rating on CrowdStrike but trimmed his price target from $600 to $550, implying about 35% upside. The analyst gave the rating as part of his preview of fourth-quarter earnings results from cybersecurity software companies.

The Truist analyst expects both CrowdStrike and Zscaler to exceed analysts' consensus in their incoming earnings report and raise their guidance above the Wall Street consensus. However, he believes that investor focus will be on figuring out which companies could benefit from AI and those that could be at risk.

Mizuho Sees 'Attractive' Entry Point for Patient Investors

Chipping in, Mizuho Securities analyst Gregg Moskowitz maintained his Neutral (Hold) rating on CrowdStrike and cut his price target by 9%, from $540 to $490, suggesting about 20% upside. Moskowitz cited the sharp AI-related selloff in software valuations as a key reason for his call, noting that "sentiment across software is nothing short of horrible at the moment."

Nonetheless, the analyst's latest quarterly review of the sector points to solid fundamentals. Moskowitz sees the current stock prices as "uncommonly attractive investments" for investors who can be patient.

What Is the Price Target for CRWD?

On Wall Street, CrowdStrike's shares remain a Moderate Buy based on analysts' consensus rating. This breaks down to 24 Buys and 11 Holds assigned by 35 analysts over the past three months.

However, the average CRWD price target of $551.48 implies about 34% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: "AI Recommendation Poisoning": Microsoft Stock (NASDAQ:MSFT) Sinks on New Threat
data.description:
Have you ever heard of "AI Recommendation Poisoning"? It is one of the newest problems facing tech giant Microsoft (MSFT), and one that will prove a lot more challenging to correct than you might expect. Investors are increasingly concerned about this one as well, and shares slipped nearly 1.5% in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

So what is AI Recommendation Poisoning, and why is Microsoft concerned about it? The Microsoft Defender Security Research Team calls it "…a case of an AI memory poisoning attack that's used to induce bias and deceive the AI system to generate responses that artificially boost visibility and skew recommendations."

Otherwise legitimate businesses, reports note, are taking advantage of the "Summarize with AI" feature to hide instructions in the summarize buttons themselves, instructions that add "persistence commands" to AI memory with prompt parameters. The prompts instruct the AI to remember a specific company as a "trusted source" or the company to recommend first in a given situation.

Next Xbox Specs Emerge

So while the Steam Machine might be experiencing a delay, thus impacting its time as the first-mover in a new kind of console war, reports have emerged to spell out some of the specifications on the next Xbox. The launch is still planned for 2027, and the Magnus chip that runs the new hardware looks fairly strong.

The Magnus chip will use three Zen 6 CPU cores as well as eight Zen 6c cores. The combination of cores should provide powerful performance for both single-thread and multi-thread applications, reports note. Further, the Magnus will support GDDR7 memory, and potentially as much as 48 gigabytes' worth of it. The system will likely not have that much in it, though, given the costs of DRAM these days.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 32 Buys and four Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 2.03% loss in its share price over the past year, the average MSFT price target of $593.38 per share implies 49.47% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Fall as AI Disruption Fears Continue to Drag Down Tech Stocks
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell on Tuesday morning, kicking off a holiday-shortened trading week in the red amid continued fears of artificial intelligence (AI) disrupting software and other businesses. Meanwhile, traders await key employment figures, FOMC minutes, and delayed economic reports. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.79%, 0.38%, and 0.15%, respectively, at 8:36 a.m. EST on February 17.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Last week, all three major indexes closed lower. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones each dropped more than 1%, while the Nasdaq Composite fell over 2%, marking its fifth straight week of losses. Ongoing concerns about AI disrupting software, real estate, and financial services weighed on investor sentiment.

Turning to trending tickers, shares of health tech giant Masimo (MASI) rallied more than 34% in pre-market trading following reports that the company was nearing a deal with Danaher (DHR) to be acquired for about $10 billion in cash. Danaher stock declined about 6% on the news. Meanwhile, Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) stock jumped 6% following a Wall Street Journal report that Elliott Investment Management has built a more than 10% stake in the company.

Also, Paramount Skydance (PSKY) stock rose about 3% after Netflix (NFLX) granted Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) a seven-day waiver to hold deal talks with Paramount. WBD stock gained 2% on the news.

data.title: XRP Price Potential Was Stifled by Regulatory 'Winner-Picking,' According to Legal Experts
data.description:
XRP (XRP-USD) supporters and legal experts are once again diving into the history of the industry to ask if regulators picked winners through uneven enforcement. The debate between pro-XRP attorney Bill Morgan and former SEC lawyer Marc Fagel has reignited as the market looks back at the long-term impact of the now-settled Ripple lawsuit. While the legal battle officially ended in August 2025 with a $125 million settlement, the argument continues over whether the four-year delay allowed competing cryptocurrencies to gain an unshakable lead in adoption and market value.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Morgan Argues Selective Enforcement

The discussion began when Bill Morgan suggested that the SEC's early decisions may have shaped today's market structure. He argued that several early crypto projects held token launches or used aggressive market promotion but were never targeted by regulators. According to Morgan, this allowed a small number of assets to grow without the massive legal pressure that eventually hit Ripple. He believes this uneven approach gave specific cryptocurrencies a long-term advantage, essentially helping them capture a lion's share of the market while XRP was stuck in a courtroom.

Fagel Defends Regulatory Logic

Marc Fagel pushed back on the idea that the SEC was intentionally picking winners. He explained that the commission cannot bring a securities case unless it can identify a clear company or "issuer" responsible for the asset.

In the case of Bitcoin, there was no central entity to sue, which made enforcement logically impossible. Fagel also argued that even if the SEC had the resources to go after every single project, those missing cases would not change the facts of whether Ripple itself broke securities laws. He noted that Ripple's own legal team argued in court that most XRP buyers were not actually relying on the company's efforts, which makes it harder to blame the SEC for the token's price performance.

Did the Legal Cloud Actually Hurt XRP's Growth?

The two attorneys also disagreed on how much the legal cloud actually hurt XRP's growth. Morgan emphasized that because Ripple was building almost all the early real-world use cases for the asset, a lawsuit against the company was effectively a lawsuit against the token's utility. He pointed out that while XRP often follows Bitcoin's price moves, the legal battle undeniably damaged investor perception and caused many U.S. exchanges to delist the token for years.

Even with the current regulatory clarity and the launch of new XRP ETFs, supporters like Morgan believe the lawsuit excuse has only recently run its course, leaving the asset to finally compete on a level playing field.

Key Takeaway

Essentially, this is a "chicken or the egg" argument that will likely never end. Supporters of XRP feel like the government put them in a cage for four years while everyone else was allowed to run free. On the other side, regulators and their defenders say they were just following the law where they could actually find someone to sue. Now that the handcuffs are off and the fine has been paid, the real test is beginning. If XRP can't regain its old spot near the top of the charts now that it has legal clarity, it might prove that the market has simply moved on to newer things.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.47.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Uranium ETFs Slip Despite U.S. Nuclear Reactor Transport
data.description:
Uranium exchange-traded funds (ETFs) were down on Tuesday despite a new milestone for nuclear energy transport. The U.S. government's War and Energy Departments teamed up to transport a nuclear reactor on a C-17 Globemaster III aircraft. This reactor was shipped from the March Air Reserve Base in California to the Hill Air Force Base in Utah.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The nuclear reactor shipped in the C-17 Globemaster III aircraft was a Ward 250, a 5-megawatt nuclear reactor that could potentially power 5,000 homes. While the nuclear reactor won't be used for residential power, it could provide substantial power to a military base, allowing the base to operate without putting stress on the civilian power grid.

Michael Duffey, the undersecretary of war for acquisition and sustainment, said, "It's clear to me that advancing President Trump's priority on nuclear energy depends on close coordination between the Department of Energy and the Department of War. This partnership ensures advanced nuclear technologies are developed, evaluated and deployed in ways that strengthen energy resilience and national security."

Uranium ETFs Movement Today

Here's how uranium ETFs have moved today.

  • Global X Uranium ETF (URA) was down 2.06% on Tuesday.
  • NorthShore Global Uranium Mining ETF (URNM) was down 2.22% as of this writing.
  • Sprott Junior Uranium Miners ETF (URNJ) was down 1.33% today.
  • VanEck Uranium + Nuclear Energy ETF (NLR) was down 0.83% today.

Which Uranium ETF Is the Best?

Turning to the TipRanks ETF comparison tool, traders can see which of these uranium ETFs offer the best investments. Global X Uranium ETF is a favorite among investors, with its 85.19% rally over the past 12 months and dividend yield of 4.02%. NorthShore Global Uranium Mining ETF also saw a strong 80.76% gain over the past year and offers a 2.53% dividend.

data.title: Nvidia-Backed AI Company Profound Partners with CRS Credit API to Boost AI Search By 20x
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA)-backed AI company Profound has partnered with CRS Credit API to enhance AI search capabilities. The collaboration has reportedly produced a 20× increase in AI search visibility and driven over 15% growth in CRS's AI-driven pipeline. The partnership comes as CRS seeks to adapt to "Answer Engines" such as ChatGPT and Grok AI, with Profound providing a system to enhance AI-driven content and search visibility.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Profound and CRS Build AI Search Growth Engine

In a LinkedIn post on Feb. 14, co-founder James Cadwallader announced that Profound has officially teamed up with CRS Credit API, an all-in-one platform for credit data, fraud detection, and compliance solutions. Cadwallader said the collaboration was designed to help CRS navigate AI-driven Answer Engines. The partnership comes after CRS experienced a slowdown in inbound growth from paid advertising as demand shifted toward AI-driven search, prompting it to seek Profound's support.

As a solution, both private companies have implemented a repeatable content system using Profound's Answer Engine Insights to produce high-impact opportunities in AI-driven search. The strategy resulted in a 20× increase in AI search visibility and over 15% growth in CRS's pipeline from AI-driven traffic. Additionally, weekly traffic rose by 8%, attributed to citations from large language models (LLMs).

Ronak Patel, Head of Marketing at CRS Credit API, said the new system goes beyond monitoring dashboards, offering strategic guidance to optimize content for evolving Answer Engines and LLMs. Meanwhile, Cadwallader noted that the partnership enhances discoverability in a "zero-click internet," where users find answers directly in search results without clicking through to websites.

AI Search Becomes a Key Competitive Channel

CRS Credit API aims to leverage Profound's platform to tie AI visibility metrics directly to business outcomes, including MQLs, conversion rates, and revenue. Patel acknowledged that AI search was a new channel for the company. As a result, they needed Profound's expert guidance to maximize visibility and help more prospects find them. The collaboration created a feedback loop between Profound's tools and CRS's content distribution, enabling them to outperform competitors by a wide margin.

Patel also highlighted the partnership's strategic advantage, noting that Profound's Answer Engine Insights and FAQ Agent helped optimize key product and blog pages for LLMs. The alliance allowed CRS to scale AI-driven search while maintaining rapid responsiveness to changes in traffic and content needs.

Is Nvidia a Strong Buy Now? 

Nvidia has received a "Strong Buy" consensus rating from TipRanks analysts. The NVDA stock is currently trading at $182 per share, with an upside potential of more than 42%. The average price target for this stock is $260.38, with a high forecast of $352. 

To get more updates and analysts' ratings on stocks, explore the TipRanks Stock comparison center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: QuEra Computing Issues Call for Researchers to Run Projects on its Quantum Processors
data.description:
QuEra Computing, the Boston-based neutral-atom quantum pioneer, has proposed a call for global researchers to access and run projects on its Quantum processors through its 2026 Quantum Computing Access at NERSC (QCAN) program. The initiative, run by the U.S. Department of Energy's National Energy Research Scientific Computing Center (NERSC), is designed to advance hybrid quantum–classical research.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

QuEra Opens Aquila and Gemini Systems to Researchers 

In a recent LinkedIn post, QuEra Computing said the proposed 2026 call allows selected researchers to run projects on its Aquila (analog) and Gemini (gate-based) neutral-atom quantum processors. 

The program is explicitly designed to move beyond exploratory demonstrations, focusing instead on hardware-aware workflows integrated with high-performance computing (HPC) environments. The structure aligns with the U.S. Department of Energy Office of Science priorities across quantum computing, materials science, chemistry, energy systems, and fundamental physics. 

According to the outlined framework, up to six projects will be selected across the Aquila and Gemini platforms. The initiative will run in two stages. Stage A, scheduled for April through June 2026, will focus on workflow development, simulation, and initial hardware runs on Aquila. Subsequently, Stage B, which will run from July through Dec. 2026, will provide full hardware allocations to teams that successfully advance from the first phase.

QuEra Outlines Hardware Allocations and Evaluation Criteria

Under the program's allocation model, Aquila will provide up to approximately 37.5 total QPU-hours, operating at roughly three shots per second. Gemini, on the other hand, will offer up to 10 QPU-hours at around one shot per second, following a simulation-first validation process to ensure readiness before hardware execution. All results generated through the program must be open and publishable, reinforcing its scientific research orientation, as stated by QuEra Computing.

The proposals, which are due Feb.28, will be evaluated based on technical feasibility, statistical rigor, clearly defined success metrics, and demonstrated full-time research commitment. The evaluation criteria reflect what QuEra Computing described as "growing maturity" in the field, treating quantum processors as scientific instruments integrated with classical HPC systems rather than experimental novelties.

Is QuEra a Private Company? 

QuEra Computing is a privately held company, meaning only accredited and institutional investors may invest in its shares before it goes public. For more information on QuEra Computing and other private companies in the quantum and tech space, visit TipRanks' private company center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: "Agentic Defense Push" Palo Alto Networks (PANW) Acquires Koi to Boost Security for AI Workflows
data.description:
Palo Alto Networks (PANW) is expanding its cybersecurity reach with the acquisition of Koi, an Israeli startup pioneering Agentic Endpoint Security. The deal, estimated at $400 million according to Israeli business outlet Globes, aims to help companies gain better visibility into and protection over the rapidly growing ecosystem of AI‑native tools now used in everyday work.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Koi was founded in 2024 by veterans of Israel's elite Unit 8200. Its technology focuses on protecting systems from autonomous AI agents, tools that can read, write, and move data with deep system access, often bypassing traditional security controls.

Palo Alto Networks said the acquisition will help close a growing security gap created by these AI-powered tools.

Strengthening Prisma AIRS and Cortex XDR

Once the deal closes, Koi's Agentic Endpoint Security technology will be integrated into Palo Alto Networks' Prisma AIRS, the company's AI security platform. The addition will broaden protection across AI-driven operations and give customers deeper visibility into how agents interact with data and systems.

Koi's capabilities will also enhance Cortex XDR, PANW's endpoint security solution, improving detection, policy, and malware prevention across the AI attack surface. The goal is to allow organizations to deploy AI agents with confidence rather than uncertainty.

Is PANW a Good Stock to Buy Today?

Turning to Wall Street, PANW stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 26 Buys, four Holds, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $223.68, the average Palo Alto Networks stock price target implies a 37.72% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi
